name: editorial
description: Structural bias for long-form, content-first pages where depth of argument and editorial authority build trust before action.
intent: Lead with a strong orientation, give the reader room to follow a sustained argument, and defer commitment requests until credibility is established.

sectionOrder.reason: Editorial pages need a clear statement of subject and authority before the reader commits time to reading.
Context and framing earn the reader's investment before the core argument arrives.
Long-form content should progress through structured sections rather than fragment into unordered cards.
Evidence in editorial pages earns authority through specificity, not social-proof density.
Reader actions should follow earned trust, not interrupt the reading experience.

density.reason: Editorial pages reward focused reading; dense layouts compete with the sustained attention required.

ctaCadence.secondaryActionRule: Surface secondary actions only as navigation aids to related depth, not as commitment shortcuts.
ctaCadence.reason: Interrupting an editorial argument with CTAs undermines the credibility the page is building.

overlayUse.reason: Overlays break reading flow and should serve reference expansion, not lead capture in content-first pages.

antiPatterns.name: cta-interrupting-argument
antiPatterns.reason: Commitment requests placed mid-argument signal that the page does not trust its own content.

antiPatterns.name: card-grid-substituting-for-narrative
antiPatterns.reason: Card grids fragment editorial arguments that depend on sequence and development.

antiPatterns.name: proof-before-context
antiPatterns.reason: Social proof placed before the reader understands the subject reads as noise.

antiPatterns.name: dense-hero-for-long-form
antiPatterns.reason: Editorial heroes should orient, not compress the whole argument into the first viewport.
